evals.prompt: I just landed a new freelance contract with a company called Acme. I need to set up a completely separate Git identity for them — their GitHub org is acme-corp and they want me to use dev@acme-corp.com as my commit email. I already have my personal and one other work account configured. Can you add the new environment for me?
evals.expected_output: Runs add-env.sh (or dry-run) with --env acme --email dev@acme-corp.com --provider github. Creates ~/repos/acme-repos/, ~/.gitconfig-acme, appends includeIf to ~/.gitconfig, appends Host github-acme to ~/.ssh/config. Prints post-setup instructions including how to upload the public key and how to clone using github-acme:<org>/<repo>.git.
expectations: The script is invoked with --env acme and --email dev@acme-corp.com
The provider used is github, resulting in SSH host alias 'github-acme'
The output includes the repos directory path ~/repos/acme-repos/
The output includes a clone example using the alias 'github-acme'
The output reminds the user to upload the public SSH key to GitHub

evals.prompt: I need to add an Azure DevOps environment for a client called Globex. My email there is dev@globex.com. I don't have an SSH key yet so please generate one too. Use the key name id_ed25519_globex.
evals.expected_output: Runs add-env.sh with --env globex --email dev@globex.com --provider azure --key id_ed25519_globex --gen-key. Creates repos folder, gitconfig, SSH config Host alias azure-globex. Generates the key. Prints clone example using azure-globex:v3/<org>/<project>/<repo>.
expectations: The script is invoked with --provider azure, resulting in SSH host alias 'azure-globex'
The clone example uses the format azure-globex:v3/<org>/<project>/<repo>
The output includes instructions to upload the key to Azure DevOps user settings
The SSH Host HostName is ssh.dev.azure.com

evals.prompt: I want to preview what would happen if I added a new git environment for a company called 'Initech' with email dev@initech.io on GitHub, key id_ed25519_initech. Don't actually change anything yet.
evals.expected_output: Runs add-env.sh with --dry-run. All steps are printed as [DRY-RUN] actions. No files are actually created or modified. The output shows what would be added to ~/.gitconfig, ~/.ssh/config, etc.
expectations: Output contains [DRY-RUN] markers indicating no changes were applied
The SSH host alias shown is 'github-initech'
The includeIf block for ~/repos/initech-repos/ is shown in the dry-run output
No actual files are created (only simulated)
